title: Pediatric headache follow-up

red_flags.text: Supports sudden onset, neurological deficit, fever, trauma, pattern change, unexplained vomiting, further investigation or referral, and structured headache-diary fields.
red_flags.rationale: Supports headache concern and diary fields from age 12 for child headache follow-up; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports sudden onset, neurological deficit, fever, trauma, pattern change, unexplained vomiting, further investigation or referral, and structured headache-diary fields.

source.title: Headaches in over 12s: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — assessment, red flags, and headache diary

red_flags.text: Supports recording headache frequency, duration, severity, intervention effectiveness, impact, and clinician-led further investigation or referral when red flags develop.
red_flags.rationale: Supports frequency, severity, impact and clinician-led referral review for child headache follow-up; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports recording headache frequency, duration, severity, intervention effectiveness, impact, and clinician-led further investigation or referral when red flags develop.

medication.text: Supports headache location, quality, severity, duration, frequency, activity impact, nausea, vomiting, light or sound sensitivity, aura, menstrual relation, and medication-overuse review.

medication.evidence_extract: Supports headache location, quality, severity, duration, frequency, activity impact, nausea, vomiting, light or sound sensitivity, aura, menstrual relation, and medication-overuse review.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Age, diagnosis, onset, frequency, duration, severity, associated and neurological symptoms, medicines, examination, diary and clinician plan are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for child headache follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
